title: Build a complete real-time inventory sync system from scratch
description:
Design and implement a full-stack real-time inventory synchronization system for the multi-store POS.

This is a greenfield architecture task that spans multiple modules:

1. Inventory Service - new service tracking stock levels per product per store, with batch operations, stock transfers between stores, and adjustment history.

2. Real-time WebSocket Layer - when inventory changes at one store (sale, restock, transfer), all connected clients see updates in real-time. Needs pub/sub architecture.

3. Conflict Resolution - when two stores sell the last item simultaneously, handle with optimistic locking and retry logic.

4. Stock Alert System - configurable low-stock thresholds per product triggering notifications. Background job scheduling.

5. Reporting Dashboard - new screen showing inventory levels across stores, stock movement history, alerts. Web and mobile views.

6. API Gateway Changes - WebSocket upgrade handling, auth for persistent connections, rate limiting.

This requires end-to-end architecture design before implementation.

title: Use Authenticated Fetcher for Category Delete
description: Update the `screen.tsx` file located in `packages/app/dashboard/categories/`. The `useSWRMutation` hook currently configured for the DELETE operation on a category uses a bare `fetcher`. Replace this with `fetcherWithToken`. This change ensures the `Authorization` header is automatically appended to the DELETE request using the current auth token from `useUserStore`. The implementation must ensure the endpoint no longer fails with a 401 Unauthorized error. Verify that the mutation correctly references the target category ID in the request payload/body if required by the API spec.

title: Add Query Invalidation to Category Add Dialog
description: Update the `AddCategoryDialog.tsx` component located in `packages/app/dashboard/categories/dialogs/`. The existing `useMutation` hook for adding categories is missing an `onSuccess` callback. Import `useQueryClient` from `@tanstack/react-query`. Inside the `useMutation` options object, add an `onSuccess` property. Set the value of this property to a function that calls `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['categories'] })`. This ensures that upon successful mutation completion, the global cache for the categories list query is invalidated, triggering a re-fetch and display update on the consuming screen.

title: Fix categories DELETE to use authenticated fetcher
description: Fix the DELETE endpoint for categories in packages/app/dashboard/categories/screen.tsx to properly send authentication headers. The current useSWRMutation for the DELETE action is using a bare fetcher instead of the authenticated fetcherWithToken. Replace the fetcher implementation to use fetcherWithToken so the Authorization header is automatically included with the request. This ensures only authenticated users can delete categories and prevents unauthenticated deletion attacks.

title: Implement PATCH /orders/:id/complete API endpoint
description: Create an API route at PATCH /orders/:id/complete that completes an order when a cashier confirms cash payment. This endpoint should set the order status to 'completed', set payment_method to 'cash', and set paid_at to the current timestamp. title: Add theme selector to dashboard settings
description:
Add a theme selector dropdown to the dashboard settings page (apps/dashboard-web). Requirements:

1. Create a new screen at packages/app/dashboard/settings/screen.tsx
2. Add a page wrapper at apps/dashboard-web/pages/settings.tsx
3. Use the existing @radix-ui/colors theme system (packages/ui/src/theme) to let shop owners pick a color theme
4. Store selected theme via the existing useShop API hook
5. Add a navigation link to settings in the DashboardLayout sidebar

IMPORTANT: All code must be TypeScript (.tsx). Use React Native components (View, Text, Pressable), NOT HTML. Follow existing patterns in the codebase.

---

BOARD REVIEW (MANDATORY):

1. Theme selection is frontend-only. It is NOT persisted anywhere. When the user refreshes, the theme resets. The selected theme MUST be saved to the shop record in the database via the API.
2. Theme is a shop-wide setting, not per-user. Only the shop admin (owner) can change the theme. All users of that shop see the same theme.

Required changes:
* Add a themeId column to the shops table in the API (D1 database via Kysely migration)
* Add a PATCH /shop/theme endpoint that updates the shop themeId (admin-only, use withAuthenticatedUser middleware)
* Update the useShop API hook in packages/app/apiHooks/useShop.ts to expose the theme and a mutation to update it
* The settings screen must call the API to persist the theme, not just set local state
* On app load, the selected theme should be fetched from the shop record and applied via ThemeProvider

Do NOT just store it in localStorage or useState. It must round-trip through the API and database.

title: Add search bar to items list page

Use a TextInput from @shop-diary/ui with a search icon (lucide-react-native Search icon). Filter by item name and description. Show a "No results" message when nothing matches. Follow existing patterns in packages/app and the dashboard screen convention.

title: Build a CRUD API for Cash Shifts
description:
We need a new service for managing cash shifts in the POS system.

A cash shift represents a cashier's work session at a register. Each shift should track:
- The user who opened it (userId, required, references users table)
- Opening amount in the register (required, decimal)
- Closing amount (optional until the shift is closed, decimal)
- Expected amount calculated by the system (optional, decimal)
- Difference between expected and actual (optional, decimal)
- Status which can be open, closed, or reconciled
- Optional notes for the cashier

This needs full CRUD: list all shifts, get one by ID, create, update, and delete.

title: Fix AddCategoryDialog query invalidation on mutation success
description: Fix the AddCategoryDialog component in packages/app/dashboard/categories/screen.tsx so that the categories list refreshes after adding a new category. The current mutation handler lacks a queryClient.invalidateQueries call in its onSuccess callback. Add useQueryClient hook and call queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["categories"] }) in the onSuccess callback of the useSWRMutation for the add category action. This ensures the cached categories query is invalidated and refetched when a new category is successfully added.

    Implement the PATCH endpoint to finalize an order transaction with cash payment logic.
    1.  **Logic:** The endpoint updates the existing `Order` record by setting the `status` to `'completed'`, `payment_method` to `'cash'`, and the `paid_at` timestamp (auto-generated or provided).
    2.  **Inventory:** It must invoke the Inventory service to deduct stock for every item included in the order. If stock is insufficient, return a `409 Conflict`.
    3.  **Data:** The request body must accept `amount_tendered` (decimal) and optionally `order_note` (string).
    4.  **Response:** Returns `200 OK` with the updated order object or error details.
*   **Schema & Zod:**
    *   `amount_tendered`: `z.number().positive()`
    *   `order_note`: `z.string().optional()`
    *   `orderNote`: `z.string().nullable()` (in Order entity)
    *   `paid_at`: `z.date().nullable()` (in Order entity)

title: Complete Order with Cash
description:
**Priority:** High
**Entity:** Order
**Route:** `PATCH /orders/:id/complete`
**Status:** `completed`, `paid`
**Type:** API Service Endpoint

**Description:**
Implement the API endpoint to finalize a cashier order. This endpoint updates the order status to completed, records the cash payment, captures the timestamp, and triggers a transaction to decrement the stock inventory for every line item in the order. If stock is insufficient for any item, the request fails with an appropriate error message. The order note is recorded as a nullable text field.

**API Fields:**
*   `status` (string, required): Enum value `"completed"`.
*   `paymentMethod` (string, required): Enum value `"cash"`.
*   `paidAt` (datetime, required): ISO 8601 timestamp of the transaction.
*   `orderNote` (string, optional): The text note from the order summary (trimmed, max 100 chars).
*   `lineItems` (array): Read-only reference to update inventory based on these items.
*   `inventory` (object): Read-only reference to decrement stock for `orderId` items.

**Logic:**
*   Verify `paymentMethod` is valid.
*   Calculate `paidAt` as current server time.
*   Decrement inventory quantity for each item in `lineItems`.
*   Save `orderNote` to the `order` table.
*   Return updated order.

title: **Key Decisions & Rationale**
description:
1. **API-First Approach**:  
   - Ticket 1 (Complete Order) is split into its own ticket to ensure the **Order entity** and **inventory logic** are handled independently from UI concerns.
   - This allows backend teams to finalize the API contract without waiting for UI designs.

2. **UI Component Separation**:  
   - Quantity controls (Ticket 2) and Remove functionality (Ticket 3) are separate to enable:
     - Independent testing (e.g., button behavior, error states).
     - Reusability (e.g., Quantity controls could be used in other contexts).
   - Note input (Ticket 4) and Summary Panel (Ticket 5) are isolated to avoid bloating a single component.

3. **Flow Logic**:  
   - Modal (Ticket 6) and Success Screen (Ticket 7) are distinct because they represent **different user states** (pre-payment vs. post-payment).
   - This prevents mixing validation logic (modal) with display logic (success screen).

4. **Field Clarity**:  
   - API ticket specifies **exact fields** (`status`, `paymentMethod`, `paidAt`, etc.) to avoid ambiguity.
   - UI tickets explicitly list **props** (e.g., `total`, `qty`, `isDisabled`) to prevent over-engineering.

5. **Inventory Decrement**:  
   - Treated as a **side effect** in Ticket 1, assuming the Inventory service exists.  
   - *If inventory doesn't exist yet*, this ticket would trigger a new epic for inventory management (but that's outside this scope).

---

title: **How to Use These Tickets**
description:
- **Backend Team**:  
  Start with **Ticket 1** to define the API contract.  
  Validate inventory logic early (e.g., check stock levels before allowing payment).
  
- **Frontend Team**:  
  Implement **UI components** (Tickets 2-5) first for rapid prototyping.  
  Add **flow logic** (Tickets 6-7) once the API is ready.

- **QA Team**:  
  Test inventory decrement in isolation (simulate low stock scenarios).  
  Validate modal behavior (e.g., confirm/cancel states).

---

title: Summary of Decomposition:
description:
1.  **API Ticket (1):** Isolated to the `Order` entity and inventory logic.
2.  **UI Tickets (2-7):** Separated into distinct components (Quantity, Remove, Note, Summary, Modal, Success).
3.  **Independence:** The API ticket depends on existing inventory logic but does not depend on specific UI state. UI tickets depend only on local state and cart context.
4.  **Field Consistency:** Fields are explicit and match the UI or API contract.
5.  **Modularity:** Quantity and Remove are separate components, allowing for independent testing and refactoring.

This decomposition provides a clear path for backend development (Ticket 1) and frontend development (Tickets 2-7) working in parallel, ensuring a robust and maintainable checkout flow.<|endoftext|><|im_start|>user

title: Build a CRUD API for Payment Types
description: Create a service for managing payment types in the POS system. Each payment type has a name (required, text), whether it is active (required, boolean as integer 0/1), a sort order (optional, integer), and a description (optional, text). This needs full CRUD: list all, get by ID, create, update, and delete.

title: Build a CRUD API for Payments
description: Create a service for recording payments against orders. Each payment tracks the order it belongs to (orderId, required, references orders table), the payment type used (paymentTypeId, required, references payment types), the amount paid (required, decimal), change given back (optional, decimal), and optional notes (text). This needs full CRUD with validation for positive amounts.

title: Build a CRUD API for Cash Shifts
description: Create a service for managing cash shifts in the POS system. A cash shift represents a cashier's work session. Each shift tracks the user who opened it (userId, required, references users table), opening amount (required, decimal), closing amount (nullable until closed, decimal), expected amount (optional, decimal), difference amount (nullable, calculated as closing - expected), status which can be open, closed, or reconciled, and optional notes. This needs full CRUD with validation for amount constraints.

title: Build a CRUD API for Cash Adjustments
description: Create a service for recording cash adjustments during a shift. Each adjustment belongs to a cash shift (cashShiftId, required, references cash shifts), has a type that can be either add or remove, an amount (required, decimal), a reason (required, text), and optional notes. This needs full CRUD with validation for positive amounts and valid adjustment types.

title: Make a theming feature
description:
Add a theme selector dropdown to the dashboard settings page (apps/dashboard-web). Requirements:

1. Create a new screen at packages/app/dashboard/settings/screen.tsx
2. Add a page wrapper at apps/dashboard-web/pages/settings.tsx
3. Use the existing @radix-ui/colors theme system (packages/ui/src/theme) to let shop owners pick a color theme
4. Store selected theme via the existing useShop API hook
5. Add a navigation link to settings in the DashboardLayout sidebar

IMPORTANT: All code must be TypeScript (.tsx). Use React Native components (View, Text, Pressable), NOT HTML. Follow existing patterns in the codebase.

***

BOARD REVIEW (MANDATORY):

1. Theme selection is frontend-only. It is NOT persisted anywhere. When the user refreshes, the theme resets. The selected theme MUST be saved to the shop record in the database via the API.
2. Theme is a shop-wide setting, not per-user. Only the shop admin (owner) can change the theme. All users of that shop see the same theme.

Required changes:

* Add a themeId column to the shops table in the API (D1 database via Kysely migration)
* Add a PATCH /shop/theme endpoint that updates the shop themeId (admin-only, use withAuthenticatedUser middleware)
* Update the useShop API hook in packages/app/apiHooks/useShop.ts to expose the theme and a mutation to update it
* The settings screen must call the API to persist the theme, not just set local state
* On app load, the selected theme should be fetched from the shop record and applied via ThemeProvider

Do NOT just store it in localStorage or useState. It must round-trip through the API and database.
